devanagari: शर्यातिवंशः सुकन्याख्यानं रेवतकन्याख्यानं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । शर्यातिर्मानवो राजा ब्रह्मिष्ठः संबभूव ह । यो वा अंगिरसां सत्रे द्वितीयं अह ...
english_devnagari: śaryātivaṃśaḥ sukanyākhyānaṃ revatakanyākhyānaṃ caśrīśuka uvācaśaryātirmānavo rājā brahmiṣṭhaḥ saṃbabhūva hayo vā aṃgirasāṃ satre dvitīyaṃ aha ūcivān
translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
It is reported that Manu’s son, king Śaryāti was the master of the Vedic lore and it was he who, in the sacrificial session of the Aṅgiras, authoritatively explained the procedure of the ritualistic course of the second day (of that session).

devanagari: सुकन्या नाम तस्यासीत् कन्या कमललोचना । तया सार्धं वनगतो हि अगमत् व्यवनाश्रमम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sukanyā nāma tasyāsīt kanyā kamalalocanātayā sārdhaṃ vanagato hi agamat vyavanāśramam
translation: He had a daughter Sukanyā by name, whose eyes were beautiful like lotuses. Accompanied by her, he went to the hermitage of the sage Cyavana.

devanagari: सा सखीभिः परिवृता विचिन्वन्त्यंघ्रिपान् वने । वल्मीकरन्ध्रे ददृशे खद्योते इव ज्योतिषी ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sā sakhībhiḥ parivṛtā vicinvantyaṃghripān vanevalmīkarandhre dadṛśe khadyote iva jyotiṣī
translation: Surrounded by her maids (of honour), she was leisurely wandering in the forest enjoying the sight of beautiful trees, when she happened to notice a pair of glow-worm-like luminous objects, in the hole of an ant-hill.

devanagari: ते दैवचोदिता बाला ज्योतिषी कण्टकेन वै । अविध्यन् मुग्धभावेन सुस्रावासृक् ततो बहिः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: te daivacoditā bālā jyotiṣī kaṇṭakena vaiavidhyan mugdhabhāvena susrāvāsṛk tato bahiḥ
translation: Impelled by her fate, she, in her childish innocence, perforated the pair of shining objects with a thorn, and blood flowed profusely out of them.

devanagari: शकृत् मूत्रनिरोधोऽभूत् सैनिकानां च तत्क्षणात् । राजर्षिः तं उपालक्ष्य पुरुषान् विस्मितोऽब्रवीत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: śakṛt mūtranirodho'bhūt sainikānāṃ ca tatkṣaṇātrājarṣiḥ taṃ upālakṣya puruṣān vismito'bravīt
translation: That very moment instantly, there was obstruction in the lower passage of his soldiers (stoppage of the excretion and urination). Observing that, the royal sage was astonished and enquired of his men.

devanagari: अप्यभद्रं न युष्माभिः भार्गवस्य विचेष्टितम् । व्यक्तं केनापि नस्तस्य कृतं आश्रमदूषणम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: apyabhadraṃ na yuṣmābhiḥ bhārgavasya viceṣṭitamvyaktaṃ kenāpi nastasya kṛtaṃ āśramadūṣaṇam
translation: ‘Has any of you given offence or done wrong to Cyavana, the descendant of the Bhṛgus. Obviously, someone of us must have profaned his hermitage’.

devanagari: सुकन्या प्राह पितरं भीता किञ्चित् कृतं मया । द्वे ज्योतिषी अजानन्त्या निर्भिन्ने कण्टकेन वै ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sukanyā prāha pitaraṃ bhītā kiñcit kṛtaṃ mayādve jyotiṣī ajānantyā nirbhinne kaṇṭakena vai
translation: Being terrified, Sukanyā confessed to her father. “Something has been done by me in ignorance, inasmuch as a pair of glittering objects were unknowingly pierced by me”.

devanagari: दुहितुस्तद् वचः श्रुत्वा शर्यातिर्जातसाध्वसः । मुनिं प्रसादयामास वल्मीकान्तर्हितं शनैः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: duhitustad vacaḥ śrutvā śaryātirjātasādhvasaḥmuniṃ prasādayāmāsa valmīkāntarhitaṃ śanaiḥ
translation: Hearing the words of his daughter, Śaryāti was seized with fear. He slowly tried to appease the sage, lying hidden in the ant-hill.

devanagari: तद् अभिप्रायमाज्ञाय प्रादाद् दुहितरं मुनेः । कृच्छ्रात् मुक्तः तमामंत्र्य पुरं प्रायात् समाहितः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tad abhiprāyamājñāya prādād duhitaraṃ muneḥkṛcchrāt muktaḥ tamāmaṃtrya puraṃ prāyāt samāhitaḥ
translation: Having ascertained the desire of the sage, the king gave (in marriage) his daughter to the sage. Having thus extricated himself from the scrape, and taking his leave (of the sage), he proceeded to his capital cautiously.

devanagari: सुकन्या च्यवनं प्राप्य पतिं परमकोपनम् । प्रीणयामास चित्तज्ञा अप्रमत्तानुवृत्तिभिः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sukanyā cyavanaṃ prāpya patiṃ paramakopanamprīṇayāmāsa cittajñā apramattānuvṛttibhiḥ
translation: Having obtained the extremely wrathful Cyavana as her husband, Sukanyā who (studied and) understood his mentel proclivities, won him over by meticulously careful obedience.

devanagari: कस्यचित् त्वथ कालस्य नासत्यावाश्रमागतौ । तौ पूजयित्वा प्रोवाच वयो मे दत्तमीश्वरौ ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kasyacit tvatha kālasya nāsatyāvāśramāgatautau pūjayitvā provāca vayo me dattamīśvarau
translation: After a lapse of some time after this, the twingods Aśvinikumāras visited his hermitage. Worshipping them (with due formalities as guests), he earnestly requested, “Oh ye celestial physicians! Be pleased to restore to me my youth.

devanagari: ग्रहं ग्रहीष्ये सोमस्य यज्ञे वामप्यसोमपोः । क्रियतां मे वयो रूपं प्रमदानां यदीप्सितम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: grahaṃ grahīṣye somasya yajñe vāmapyasomapoḥkriyatāṃ me vayo rūpaṃ pramadānāṃ yadīpsitam
translation: Even though you are not eligible to share the Somajuice, I shall bear you both gobletfuls of Soma in the Soma sacrifice. Be pleased to bestow on me that youthfulness and beauty, covetable to young damsels.”

devanagari: बाढं इति ऊचतुर्विप्रं अभिनन्द्य भिषक्तमौ । निमज्जतां भवान् अस्मिन् ह्रदे सिद्धविनिर्मिते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: bāḍhaṃ iti ūcaturvipraṃ abhinandya bhiṣaktamaunimajjatāṃ bhavān asmin hrade siddhavinirmite
translation: “Assuredly” approved the foremost of physicians rejoicing at his offer. “May your worship be pleased to plunge in this pool created by Siddhas.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा जरया ग्रस्त देहो धमनिसन्ततः । ह्रदं प्रवेशितोऽश्विभ्यां वलीपलित विप्रियः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā jarayā grasta deho dhamanisantataḥhradaṃ praveśito'śvibhyāṃ valīpalita vipriyaḥ
translation: With these words, Cyavana, whose body was stricken with old age and was all over covered with protuberant veins, and appeared repulsive due to his wrinkles and grey hairs, was made to enter the pool by the pair of Aśvin gods.

devanagari: पुरुषास्त्रय उत्तस्थुः अपीव्या वनिताप्रियाः । पद्मस्रजः कुण्डलिनः तुल्यरूपाः सुवाससः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: puruṣāstraya uttasthuḥ apīvyā vanitāpriyāḥpadmasrajaḥ kuṇḍalinaḥ tulyarūpāḥ suvāsasaḥ
translation: There emerged three men (from that pool)—all bewitchingly beautiful, captivating to women, adorned with lotus-garlands and ear-rings, clad in rich garments, resembling each other in form.

devanagari: तान् निरीक्ष्य वरारोहा सरूपान् सूर्यवर्चसः । अजानती पतिं साध्वी अश्विनौ शरणं ययौ ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tān nirīkṣya varārohā sarūpān sūryavarcasaḥajānatī patiṃ sādhvī aśvinau śaraṇaṃ yayau
translation: Finding them all resembling each other in form, resplendent like the sun, the chaste lady Sukanyā, being unable to distinguish as to who her husband was, sought refuge with Aśvinikumāras (to help her in knowing the husband, by standing apart).

devanagari: दर्शयित्वा पतिं तस्यै पातिव्रत्येन तोषितौ । ऋषिमामन्त्र्य ययतुः विमानेन त्रिविष्टपम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: darśayitvā patiṃ tasyai pātivratyena toṣitauṛṣimāmantrya yayatuḥ vimānena triviṣṭapam
translation: Being pleased with her fidelity to her husband, the Aśvinikumāras pointed out her husband. Taking leave of the sage, they left for the celestial region, in their heavenly car.

devanagari: यक्ष्यमाणोऽथ शर्यातिः च्यवनस्याश्रमं गतः । ददर्श दुहितुः पार्श्वे पुरुषं सूर्यवर्चसम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: yakṣyamāṇo'tha śaryātiḥ cyavanasyāśramaṃ gataḥdadarśa duhituḥ pārśve puruṣaṃ sūryavarcasam
translation: One day, king Śaryāti, intending to perform a sacrifice, went to the hermitage of Cyavana, and found a man glorious like the Sun, by the side of his daughter.

devanagari: राजा दुहितरं प्राह कृतपादाभिवन्दनाम् । आशिषश्चाप्रयुञ्जानो नातिप्रीतिमना इव ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: rājā duhitaraṃ prāha kṛtapādābhivandanāmāśiṣaścāprayuñjāno nātiprītimanā iva
translation: When the daughter bowed down to him (to pay respect), the king was not much pleased in his mind about her, and instead of giving the usual benedictions, censured her.

devanagari: चिकीर्षितं ते किमिदं पतिस्त्वया । प्रलम्भितो लोकनमस्कृतो मुनिः । यत्त्वं जराग्रस्तमसत्यसम्मतं । विहाय जारं भजसेऽमुमध्वगम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: cikīrṣitaṃ te kimidaṃ patistvayāpralambhito lokanamaskṛto muniḥyattvaṃ jarāgrastamasatyasammataṃvihāya jāraṃ bhajase'mumadhvagam
translation: “What have you intended to do? Your husband, a sage respectfully saluted by all the world, is deceived by you, inasmuch as abandoninig the aged husband falsely approved by you, you have resorted to a way-farer as your paramour, you unchaste woman!

devanagari: कथं मतिस्तेऽवगतान्यथा सतां । कुलप्रसूते कुलदूषणं त्विदम् । बिभर्षि जारं यदपत्रपा कुलं । पितुश्च भर्तुश्च नयस्यधस्तमः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: kathaṃ matiste'vagatānyathā satāṃkulaprasūte kuladūṣaṇaṃ tvidambibharṣi jāraṃ yadapatrapā kulaṃpituśca bhartuśca nayasyadhastamaḥ
translation: How is it that your mentality stooped to such low wickedness? Otherwise, you are born in a noble family of righteous persons, and this is a vile stigma on the family. You have lost your sense of shame and are harbouring a paramour, hurling thereby the families of your father and husband into the dark depth of hell.”

devanagari: एवं ब्रुवाणं पितरं स्मयमाना शुचिस्मिता । उवाच तात जामाता तवैष भृगुनन्दनः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ bruvāṇaṃ pitaraṃ smayamānā śucismitāuvāca tāta jāmātā tavaiṣa bhṛgunandanaḥ
translation: To her father who was thus indicting her, Sukanyā. of pure gentle smiles, replied slightly laughting, “Father! This is your son-in-law Cyavana, the delight (or descendant) of Bhṛgu”.

devanagari: शशंस पित्रे तत् सर्वं वयोरूपाभिलम्भनम् । विस्मितः परमप्रीतः तनयां परिषस्वजे ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śaśaṃsa pitre tat sarvaṃ vayorūpābhilambhanamvismitaḥ paramaprītaḥ tanayāṃ pariṣasvaje
translation: Then she reported to her father how he (Cyavana) came to regain his youth and beautiful looks. Full of astonishment and being extremely pleased with her, he embraced his daughter.

devanagari: सोमेन याजयन् वीरं ग्रहं सोमस्य चाग्रहीत् । असोमपोः अपि अश्विनोः च्यवनः स्वेन तेजसा ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: somena yājayan vīraṃ grahaṃ somasya cāgrahītasomapoḥ api aśvinoḥ cyavanaḥ svena tejasā
translation: Cyavana made the heroic king Śaryāti to perform a Soma sacrifice, in which by dint of his personal ascetic powers, he bore a cup of Soma to the pair of Aśvinikumāras, even though they were not entitled for a share in Soma till then.

devanagari: हन्तुं तमाददे वज्रं सद्यो मन्युरमर्षितः । सवज्रं स्तम्भयामास भुजं इन्द्रस्य भार्गवः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: hantuṃ tamādade vajraṃ sadyo manyuramarṣitaḥsavajraṃ stambhayāmāsa bhujaṃ indrasya bhārgavaḥ
translation: The irascible Indra was instantly enraged and raised his Vajra (thunderbolt) to kill him. But Cyavana, a descendant of Bhṛgus, petrified Indra’s arm along with the Vajra.

devanagari: अन्वजानन् ततः सर्वे ग्रहं सोमस्य चाश्विनोः । भिषजाविति यत् पूर्वं सोमाहुत्या बहिष्कृतौ ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: anvajānan tataḥ sarve grahaṃ somasya cāśvinoḥbhiṣajāviti yat pūrvaṃ somāhutyā bahiṣkṛtau
translation: Thenceforth, all of them (gods) agreed to give a share in Soma juice to Aśvinikumāras who, being physicians, were previously excluded from the oblations of Soma to them.

devanagari: उत्तानबर्हिरानर्तो भूरिषेण इति त्रयः । शर्यातेरभवन् पुत्रा आनर्ताद् रेवतोऽभवत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: uttānabarhirānarto bhūriṣeṇa iti trayaḥśaryāterabhavan putrā ānartād revato'bhavat
translation: Śaryāti had three sons—Uttānabarhis, Ānarta and Bhūriṣeṇa. Revata was born of Anarta.

devanagari: सोऽन्तःसमुद्रे नगरीं विनिर्माय कुशस्थलीम् । आस्थितोऽभुङ्क्त विषयान् आनर्तादीन् अरिन्दम ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: so'ntaḥsamudre nagarīṃ vinirmāya kuśasthalīmāsthito'bhuṅkta viṣayān ānartādīn arindama
translation: Building a city called Kuśasthalī, in the middle of the sea, he occupied it and ruled over the country called Ānarta (and adjoining territories), Oh subduer of enemies.

devanagari: तस्य पुत्रशतं जज्ञे ककुद्मि ज्येष्ठमुत्तमम् । ककुद्मी रेवतीं कन्यां स्वां आदाय विभुं गतः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya putraśataṃ jajñe kakudmi jyeṣṭhamuttamamkakudmī revatīṃ kanyāṃ svāṃ ādāya vibhuṃ gataḥ
translation: One hundred excellent sons, of whom Kakudmin was the eldest, were born to him. Taking with him his daughter Revatī Kakudmī went to god Brahmā for consulting him for a suitable husband to his daughter. The region of Brahmā was then open. As a musical concert of Gandharvas, was going on, he did not get opportunity (to speak to god Brahmā) and waited for a moment.

devanagari: पुत्र्या वरं परिप्रष्टुं ब्रह्मलोकं अपावृतम् । आवर्तमाने गान्धर्वे स्थितोऽलब्धक्षणः क्षणम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: putryā varaṃ paripraṣṭuṃ brahmalokaṃ apāvṛtamāvartamāne gāndharve sthito'labdhakṣaṇaḥ kṣaṇam
translation: One hundred excellent sons, of whom Kakudmin was the eldest, were born to him. Taking with him his daughter Revatī Kakudmī went to god Brahmā for consulting him for a suitable husband to his daughter. The region of Brahmā was then open. As a musical concert of Gandharvas, was going on, he did not get opportunity (to speak to god Brahmā) and waited for a moment.

devanagari: तदन्त आद्यमानम्य स्वाभिप्रायं न्यवेदयत् । तत् श्रुत्वा भगवान् ब्रह्मा प्रहस्य तमुवाच ह ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadanta ādyamānamya svābhiprāyaṃ nyavedayattat śrutvā bhagavān brahmā prahasya tamuvāca ha
translation: After the conclusion of the musical programme, he bowed down to god Brahmā (the first created god), and told the object of his visit. Hearing that, god Brahmā laughed loudly and said.

devanagari: अहो राजन् निरुद्धास्ते कालेन हृदि ये कृताः । तत्पुत्रपौत्र नप्तॄणां गोत्राणि च न श्रृण्महे ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: aho rājan niruddhāste kālena hṛdi ye kṛtāḥtatputrapautra naptṝṇāṃ gotrāṇi ca na śrṛṇmahe
translation: “Oh King! Suitors that you had in your mind, have already been swallowed up by Time long ago. We do not even hear of their sons, grandsons, great-grandsons. Nay, not even their gotras (races).

devanagari: कालोऽभियातस्त्रिणव चतुर्युगविकल्पितः । तद्‍गच्छ देवदेवांशो बलदेवो महाबलः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: kālo'bhiyātastriṇava caturyugavikalpitaḥtad‍gaccha devadevāṃśo baladevo mahābalaḥ
translation: The time calculated as thrice nine (i.e. twenty-seven) revolutions of the group of four yugas has now elapsed. Therefore, now you go back and offer this jewel of a daughter to the extremely mighty Baladeva, the ray of the God of gods, and a jewel among men. The glorious Lord, the creater of all beings, the chanting and hearing of whose name is meritorious, has incarnated on the earth by his own aṃśa (ray) for lightening the burden of the earth”. When thus commanded, he bowed down to the unborn god Brahmā, and returned to his capital which was already abandoned by his brothers, out of fear of yakṣas, who were scattered all over the quarters (different parts of the earth).

devanagari: कन्यारत्‍नमिदं राजन् नररत्‍नाय देहि भोः । भुवो भारावताराय भगवान् भूतभावनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kanyārat‍namidaṃ rājan nararat‍nāya dehi bhoḥbhuvo bhārāvatārāya bhagavān bhūtabhāvanaḥ
translation: The time calculated as thrice nine (i.e. twenty-seven) revolutions of the group of four yugas has now elapsed. Therefore, now you go back and offer this jewel of a daughter to the extremely mighty Baladeva, the ray of the God of gods, and a jewel among men. The glorious Lord, the creater of all beings, the chanting and hearing of whose name is meritorious, has incarnated on the earth by his own aṃśa (ray) for lightening the burden of the earth”. When thus commanded, he bowed down to the unborn god Brahmā, and returned to his capital which was already abandoned by his brothers, out of fear of yakṣas, who were scattered all over the quarters (different parts of the earth).

devanagari: अवतीर्णो निजांशेन पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तनः । इत्यादिष्टोऽभिवन्द्याजं नृपः स्वपुरमागतः । त्यक्तं पुण्यजनत्रासाद्‍भ्रातृभिर्दिक्ष्ववस्थितैः ॥ ३५ ...
english_devnagari: avatīrṇo nijāṃśena puṇyaśravaṇakīrtanaḥityādiṣṭo'bhivandyājaṃ nṛpaḥ svapuramāgataḥtyaktaṃ puṇyajanatrāsād‍bhrātṛbhirdikṣvavasthitaiḥ
translation: The time calculated as thrice nine (i.e. twenty-seven) revolutions of the group of four yugas has now elapsed. Therefore, now you go back and offer this jewel of a daughter to the extremely mighty Baladeva, the ray of the God of gods, and a jewel among men. The glorious Lord, the creater of all beings, the chanting and hearing of whose name is meritorious, has incarnated on the earth by his own aṃśa (ray) for lightening the burden of the earth”. When thus commanded, he bowed down to the unborn god Brahmā, and returned to his capital which was already abandoned by his brothers, out of fear of yakṣas, who were scattered all over the quarters (different parts of the earth).

devanagari: सुतां दत्त्वानवद्याङ्गीं बलाय बलशालिने । बदर्याख्यं गतो राजा तप्तुं नारायणाश्रमम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: sutāṃ dattvānavadyāṅgīṃ balāya balaśālinebadaryākhyaṃ gato rājā taptuṃ nārāyaṇāśramam
translation: Having given in marriage his extremely beautiful daughter to the mighty Balarāma, the king repaired to the hermitage of Nārāyaṇa, known Badarī, to perform austerities.
